summary: Split from #2345 to land five gm/mn decompilation implementations in game-loop and menu code: menu rule text handling, opening fog animation gating, opening performance-label construction, match-end team-score aggregation, and bonus lookup. The diff replaced stubs with C, updated headers from UNK prototypes to typed signatures, and refined mnmainrule's struct layout. PR text claims all five functions were 100%, while the decomp.dev bot report listed three new 100% matches and two near-100 improvements, so exact final match status for fn_80165E7C and fn_801A7FB4 should be verified before reusing as a certainty.

key_files.role: Implemented mn_802308F0, a menu rule-description HSD_Text create/destroy callback.
Exposed mn_802307F8_t offset 0xA as xA, preserved pads to 0x130, and typed mn_802308F0.
Implemented fn_801A7FB4 fog animation gating and added fog.h.
Changed fn_801A7FB4 prototype to void fn_801A7FB4(HSD_GObj*).
Implemented fn_801A9FCC performance label builder with static data and callback cast in fn_801AA0E8.
Changed fn_801A9FCC prototype to void* fn_801A9FCC(void).
Implemented fn_80165E7C match-end team standings aggregation.
Changed fn_80165E7C prototype to void fn_80165E7C(MatchEnd*).
Implemented fn_8016F870 reverse bonus lookup with table flags and player-achievement checks.
Changed fn_8016F870 prototype to int fn_8016F870(void*, u16, u8, u8).

what_changed: mn_802308F0 now reads HSD_GObj user data as mn_802307F8_t, selects hovered_selection versus data->x1 based on arg1, switches on data->xA, destroys an existing HSD_Text for modes 2/4, creates text via mn_802307F8 for modes 1/3, conditionally creates text for mode 0, and leaves mode 5 as a no-op.
mnmainrule.h replaced one large pad with offset-preserving pads around the newly identified xA byte at 0xA and kept HSD_Text* text at 0x130.

fn_801A7FB4 now gets HSD_Fog from the gobj, counts 26 gm_801A659C entries that pass un_803048C0, and gates HSD_FogInterpretAnim by count thresholds <=5 and <=0xD plus fog animation frame thresholds 185.0f and 200.0f.

fn_801A9FCC now builds a static PerfLabelLine list from gm_80480B38, formats lbMthp_8001F5F4/E4/D4 metrics with gm_803DBF78 format strings, converts ticks to milliseconds using the bus clock at 0x800000F8, and returns the line array pointer.

fn_80165E7C now iterates six player standings, skips slot_type 3, aggregates player scores/subscores into team_standings, marks teams active, and handles Stamina VS or arg0->x5 == 1 with separate signed score rules.

fn_8016F870 now validates arg1, scans bonus indices downward, uses lbl_803D5A4C kind/flag data with sentinel 0x29A, applies a mask, calls pl_80039418 for generic bonuses and fn_801701C0 for special bonuses, and returns the first matching index or -1.
The PR updated all touched headers from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS-style declarations to typed signatures for the newly decompiled functions.
The PR body notes fn_8023DAEC in mnnamenew.c was dropped from the #2345 split because it was already upstream.
smart_moves: Kept the PR reviewable by splitting from #2345 and limiting scope to game-loop/menu matches instead of mixing in already-upstream mnnamenew work.
Refined mn_802307F8_t minimally: exposed only the newly needed xA field while preserving exact offset math and leaving unknown bytes as pads.
Updated prototypes in headers alongside implementations, which documents real call signatures and prevents future code from depending on UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS for these functions.
Matched fn_8016F870 with the table sentinel path and separate check labels instead of over-abstracting the duplicated generic/special lookup flow.
Preserved codegen-sensitive details such as PAD_STACK, explicit casts, u8 masking, f32/u32 conversion order, and callback casting to Event.
The squash commit documents an incompatible-pointer-types fix using casts at mn_802307F8 call sites without changing compiled assembly.
The squash commit also records a data relocation-symbol naming caveat for fn_8016F870: split-tool versus MWCC anchor names may differ while resolving to the same address.
decomp_lessons: When a user-data struct is only partly known, expose discovered fields with offset comments and calculated pads rather than guessing semantic fields.
For menu text callbacks, HSD_Text lifetime is often encoded as state-machine cases; matching may require preserving create-if-null and destroy-then-null behavior exactly.
For table-driven bonus/achievement code, model sentinel termination and branch-specific predicate calls directly before trying to deduplicate control flow.
Do not simplify repeated calls, duplicated loops, ternary boolean normalization, or apparently redundant stores until the assembly match has been verified.
Static overlay builders can require exact data layout, string offsets, and pointer arithmetic over a static block to reproduce relocations and codegen.
Replacing UNK prototypes with typed signatures is part of the match: it improves future compile-time feedback and documents argument sizes that affect casts/codegen.
PAD_STACK can appear in otherwise straightforward functions, including after a return in fn_8016F870, and should be treated as a matching artifact rather than dead-code cleanup by default.
Automated match reports and PR-body match tables can disagree; record both and verify locally before citing exact 100% status for follow-up work.
naming_conventions: Unknown global functions remained address-based, such as fn_80165E7C, fn_8016F870, fn_801A7FB4, and fn_801A9FCC.
Subsystem prefixes were preserved for known project symbols, such as mn_802308F0, mn_802307F8, gm_801A4310, and gm_801A659C.
Unknown struct fields used offset-style names like xA, x5, and x30 rather than speculative semantic names.
Pads were renamed with offset ranges, x3_pad[0x00A-0x003] and xB_pad[0x130-0x00B], to make layout constraints searchable.
Static globals used address-based names, including gm_803DBF78 and gm_80480B38.
Local variables used cautious semantic names where evidence was direct, such as team, player_score, team_score, mask, player_id, flags, count, lines, and data.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: fn_801A7FB4 uses PAD_STACK(0x10), repeats gm_801A4310 and the 26-entry count loop, and keeps un_803048C0(...) ? true : false, all of which are likely codegen-sensitive.
fn_8016F870 uses goto check1/check2 labels to share the sentinel-default flags path before mask and achievement checks.
fn_8016F870 keeps explicit casts such as (u8) mask & flags and (unsigned) fn_801701C0(...) != 0.
fn_8016F870 includes PAD_STACK(8) after return -1, indicating stack-shape matching mattered even around unreachable-looking code.
fn_801A9FCC defines a packed static block with f32 data followed by format strings at offsets 0x24 and 0x30, then accesses the strings through a u8* base pointer.
fn_801A9FCC preserves tick-to-ms conversion ordering with f32 casts and the hardware bus-clock read from *(u32*) 0x800000F8.
gmopening retains an unusual tail-store sequence in the diff, lines[2].next = &lines[3] followed immediately by lines[2].next = NULL; treat this as match-sensitive until checked against asm.
The merge commit explicitly notes that a 100% instruction byte match can still show a split-tool versus MWCC data-anchor symbol-name difference when both names resolve to the same address.
review_feedback: No inline review comments were present in the slice.
jurrejelle approved the PR with an empty review body on 2026-04-11T14:02:57Z.
decomp-dev bot reported GALE01 matched code at 62.65%, +0.02%, +852 bytes.
The bot listed three new 100% matches: fn_8016F870, fn_801A9FCC, and mn_802308F0.
The bot listed fn_80165E7C at 98.31% and fn_801A7FB4 at 99.14% as improvements, which qualifies the PR title/body claim that all five were 100%.
follow_up_queries: Check later history for whether fn_80165E7C and fn_801A7FB4 reached true 100% after the bot reported 98.31% and 99.14%.
Search for semantic renames of fn_80165E7C, fn_8016F870, fn_801A7FB4, fn_801A9FCC, and mn_802308F0.
Look for later PRs refining mn_802307F8_t fields x0, x1, x2, xA, and text or adding enum names for modes 0 through 5.
Check whether gmopening's PerfLabelLine tail assignment and gm_803DBF78/gm_80480B38 static names were later cleaned up or explained.
Search for other PRs documenting split-tool versus MWCC relocation-anchor naming mismatches.
Review PR #2345 for the broader batch context and why fn_8023DAEC in mnnamenew.c was already upstream.
